growth_records.medium: Chemically defined carbonate medium (with carbonates) under H2/CO2

up.method: calculated: stoichiometry 4H2+CO2->CH4+2H2O, qH2 = 4 x MER = 4 x 1.219

sec.method: reported mean methane evolution rate MER_mean +/- 0.076 (Table 1)
reported mean water evolution rate WER_mean +/- 0.142 (Table 1)
reported total amino acid excretion rate AAER +/- 0.019 (Table 1)
reported total lipid production rate +/- 0.001 (Table 1); ~17.7 nmol/g/h, archaeol ~0.014 umol/g/h (7%)

growth_records.cite: Taubner et al. Lipidomics and Comparative Metabolite Excretion Analysis of Methanogenic Archaea Reveal Organism-Specific Adaptations to Varying Temperatures and Substrate Concentrations. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01159-22. [PMC10134847]
growth_records.snippet: Standard growth conditions ... 65C and a medium with carbonates for M. marburgensis ... MER_mean 1.219 +/- 0.076 mmol L-1 h-1 ... WER_mean 2.485 +/- 0.142 ... total AAER 0.125 +/- 0.019 umol L-1 h-1 ... 4H2 + CO2 -> CH4 + 2H2O
growth_records.notes: Standard conditions (Table 1). MER=1.219, WER=2.485 mmol/L/h; WER/MER=2.038. H2 and CO2 uptake derived from stated stoichiometry 4H2+CO2->CH4+2H2O: qH2=4*MER=4*1.219=4.876; qCO2=MER=1.219 mmol/L/h. No specific growth rate (mu) reported; total incubation time t_inc=229.3 h and OD_end(578nm)=1.041+/-0.057 given but doubling time not derivable. Rates are volumetric (per L culture), not per gDW.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined carbonate medium (with carbonates) under H2/CO2

sec.method: reported specific archaeol production rate +/- 3.5 at 50C, 50 mL

growth_records.cite: Taubner et al. Lipidomics and Comparative Metabolite Excretion Analysis of Methanogenic Archaea Reveal Organism-Specific Adaptations to Varying Temperatures and Substrate Concentrations. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01159-22. [PMC10134847]
growth_records.snippet: the total AAER of M. marburgensis was higher at lower temperatures (50C, 0.51 +/- 0.13 umol L-1 h-1; ...). This ... is due to an extreme increase in the rates of excretion of Ala (from 2.7 +/- 0.5 nmol-1 L-1 h-1 at 65C to 311 +/- 70 nmol-1
growth_records.notes: Temperature-variant condition: M. marburgensis 50C, 50 mL, 1x/day, carbonate medium. Explicit secretion rates: total AAER 0.51 umol/L/h, Ala 311 nmol/L/h, specific archaeol 16.3 nmol/g/h. MER/WER at 50C not given numerically in text. No mu.

growth_records.medium: Standard synthetic methanogen medium (nickel-sufficient, 5 uM Ni2+)

up.method: not_quantified_in_text; gas substrate 80% H2 at 400 ml/min
not_quantified_in_text; gas substrate 20% CO2 at 400 ml/min

sec.method: not_quantified_in_text; sole catabolic product of CO2-reducing hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis (8 e- CO2->CH4)

growth_records.cite: Nomura et al. Electron flow in hydrogenotrophic methanogens under nickel limitation. Nature (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41586-025-09229-y. [PMC12350162]
growth_records.snippet: The standard medium for culture of M. marburgensis contains 6.8 g l-1 (50 mM) KH2PO4, 2.544 g l-1 (24 mM) Na2CO3, 2.12 g l-1 (40 mM) NH4Cl, 0.2 mM MgCl2.6H2O, 50 uM FeCl2.4H2O, 5 uM NiCl2.6H2O ... The temperature of the glass vessel was con
growth_records.notes: Continuous (chemostat) culture of the CO2-reducing hydrogenotrophic methanogen M. marburgensis. Full defined medium composition and gas regime given verbatim in Methods (Cultivation methods). Nickel-sufficient standard condition = 5 uM Ni2+, 50 uM Fe2+. Text states 'The specific growth rate (mu) was calculated from the dilution rate of the continuous flow rate' and Fig.1a tabulates mu (h-1) for each condition, but NO numeric mu, dilution rate or gas/flux value is present in the machine-readable text (values only in Fig.1a source data). Therefore growth_rate_per_h left null (cannot compute without an explicit number). pH 6.7 taken from the trace-element/medium adjustment ('adjusted to pH 6.7'). H2:CO2:H2S = 80:20:0.2, 400 ml/min. No qS, qP, or yield numerically stated; substrate = H2+CO2, product = CH4 (implicit).

growth_records.medium: Nickel-limiting synthetic methanogen medium (50 nM Ni2+)
growth_records.cite: Nomura et al. Electron flow in hydrogenotrophic methanogens under nickel limitation. Nature (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41586-025-09229-y. [PMC12350162]
growth_records.snippet: M. marburgensis grows relatively well even at 50 nM Ni2+, with specific growth rates reduced by around half compared to those in nickel-sufficient conditions.
growth_records.notes: Strictly nickel-limited chemostat condition (50 nM Ni2+; Fe2+ still 50 uM). Only qualitative growth statement is numerically extractable: 'specific growth rates reduced by around half compared to those in nickel-sufficient conditions' => mu(50 nM) approx 0.5 x mu(5 uM standard). No absolute numeric mu given in text (Fig.1a source data only), so growth_rate_per_h left null; a derived value would require the numeric standard mu, which is not stated. Medium identical to standard record except NiCl2 controlled to 50 nM. Additional Ni conditions 250 nM and 125 nM also cultured (proteomics) but no distinct rate data. No qS/qP/yield numerically stated.
